subtasks.description: Query MongoDB for Adobe license data including license ID, product type, quantity, cost, renewal date, and status
Query Snowflake for Adobe Q4 usage metrics including product, consumed units, utilization %, and cost
Reconcile MongoDB license data with Snowflake Q4 usage, identify discrepancies, calculate utilization variance, and generate a summary report for Slack posting
Post the reconciliation summary to the #sam-team Slack channel

plan.cost_rationale: Minimum decomposition: 4 subtasks (not 6). Two parallel fetches (Mongo and SQL) execute independently in tier-small as they are single-source, explicit queries. Cross_recon at tier-mid reconciles both sources and generates the summary—classification and reconciliation logic stay in one specialist, not split further. Multitool_plan at tier-small orchestrates the Slack post. Budget of 6 tool calls accommodates: mongo_query call + sql_gen call + cross_recon reasoning + Slack API invocation + overhead. Tiers are minimal: simple single-source fetches use small; reconciliation with two inputs uses mid; Slack posting uses small.

output.sql: SELECT product, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS consumed_units, ROUND(AVG(daily_active_seats)::numeric, 2) AS utilization_pct, SUM(amount_usd) AS cost FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Adobe' AND event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY product ORDER BY cost DESC

arguments.message: Adobe Reconciliation Summary (Mongo vs Snowflake Q4): 1 entity reconciled - adobe_mongo_vs_snowflake_q4: classification=no_data_provided (mongo_value=null, sql_value=null).
